description: baseline: T1-T4 green before adding next TDD step

asi.hypothesis: establish current v0.1 baseline before adding T5 recall_text keyword behavior
asi.notes: autoresearch.jsonl was empty despite existing T1-T4 commits, so initialized experiment and recorded clean baseline

description: T5 recall_text passed benchmark but failed fmt check

asi.hypothesis: add minimal Store::recall_text keyword search over active claim triple fields
asi.rollback_reason: cargo fmt --check required wrapping tuple destructuring in recall_text loop
asi.next_action_hint: reapply T5 test and implementation, run cargo fmt before autoresearch.sh
asi.red_evidence: cargo test -p memory-core recall_text_returns_claim_by_keyword failed with no method named recall_text
asi.implementation_notes: Used SQLite LIKE across subject/predicate/object ordered by id; no ADR changes

description: T5 adds keyword recall over active claim text

asi.hypothesis: a minimal SQLite LIKE-based Store::recall_text satisfies the v0.1 keyword recall milestone without changing storage schema
asi.red_evidence: test recall_text_returns_claim_by_keyword failed before implementation because Store had no recall_text method
asi.green_evidence: cargo test --workspace -q passed; autoresearch.sh parsed tests_green=5 tests_total=5 and checks passed
asi.adr_alignment: ADR-0008 recall surface begins with text recall; ADR-0004 HybridRAG deferred to v0.2; ADRs left unchanged

description: T6 adds memory status CLI command

asi.hypothesis: the smallest CLI skeleton step is a status command that opens/creates the configured store and reports readiness
asi.red_evidence: status_reports_store_ready_for_memory_dir initially failed because the binary printed empty stdout
asi.green_evidence: cargo test --workspace -q passed; autoresearch.sh parsed tests_green=6 tests_total=6 and checks passed
asi.implementation_notes: Added clap parser with --memory-dir and Status subcommand only; no ADR or schema changes

description: T7 adds memory remember CLI command

asi.hypothesis: memory remember can be a thin CLI wrapper over Store::add_claim with explicit subject/predicate/object/source
asi.red_evidence: after adding serde_json dev-dep for the integration test, remember_writes_claim_to_configured_store failed because remember subcommand was not implemented
asi.green_evidence: cargo test --workspace -q passed; autoresearch.sh parsed tests_green=7 tests_total=7 and checks passed
asi.implementation_notes: Added Remember subcommand and dev-dependency serde_json for log assertion; write still flows through Store::add_claim preserving log-first invariant

description: T8 adds memory recall CLI command

asi.hypothesis: the CLI recall command can reuse Store::recall_text and print one stable subject-predicate-object line per match
asi.red_evidence: recall_prints_claim_matching_keyword failed because recall was not a recognized subcommand
asi.green_evidence: cargo test --workspace -q passed; autoresearch.sh parsed tests_green=8 tests_total=8 and checks passed
asi.implementation_notes: Added Recall subcommand only; output intentionally simple and deterministic for v0.1

description: T9 starts v0.2 Ollama embedding request shape

asi.hypothesis: v0.2 can begin safely with a deterministic serializable Ollama embedding request before adding network I/O or sqlite-vss integration
asi.red_evidence: ollama_embedding_request_serializes_model_and_prompt failed because memory_core::vector did not exist
asi.green_evidence: cargo test --workspace -q passed; autoresearch.sh parsed tests_green=9 tests_total=9 milestone_index=2 and checks passed
asi.adr_alignment: ADR-0013 requires Rust HTTP to local Ollama; ADR-0004 HybridRAG vector side; book retrieval emphasized HybridRAG/vector+graph context [ch.100] and storage choice by actual walls not checklists [ch.105]

description: T10 adds Ollama embedding response parsing

asi.hypothesis: parsing the Ollama embedding response vector is the next smallest non-network v0.2 step
asi.red_evidence: ollama_embedding_response_deserializes_embedding_vector failed because OllamaEmbeddingResponse did not exist
asi.green_evidence: cargo test --workspace -q passed; autoresearch.sh parsed tests_green=10 tests_total=10 and checks passed
asi.implementation_notes: Added Deserialize response struct with Vec<f32>; no HTTP dependency or external calls yet

description: T11 normalizes Ollama embeddings endpoint URL

asi.hypothesis: before live HTTP, the embedding client should deterministically normalize the Ollama base URL to /api/embeddings
asi.red_evidence: ollama_embedding_client_normalizes_embeddings_endpoint_url failed because OllamaEmbeddingClient did not exist
asi.green_evidence: cargo test --workspace -q passed; autoresearch.sh parsed tests_green=11 tests_total=11 and checks passed
asi.implementation_notes: Added client storing trimmed base URL and model; model accessor included to avoid hidden dead field while preserving future request construction

description: T12 builds Ollama embedding requests from client model

asi.hypothesis: the embedding client should own model configuration and construct serializable request bodies for prompts
asi.red_evidence: ollama_embedding_client_builds_request_with_configured_model failed because OllamaEmbeddingClient had no request method
asi.green_evidence: cargo test --workspace -q passed; autoresearch.sh parsed tests_green=12 tests_total=12 and checks passed
asi.implementation_notes: Added request(&self, prompt) returning OllamaEmbeddingRequest borrowing the configured model; still no network I/O

description: T13 adds vector chunk metadata migration

asi.hypothesis: v0.2 vector storage should first persist chunk metadata in SQLite so a future sqlite-vss table can join by row/claim id
asi.red_evidence: fresh_database_has_vector_chunks_table failed because migrations did not create vector_chunks
asi.green_evidence: cargo test --workspace -q passed; autoresearch.sh parsed tests_green=13 tests_total=13 and checks passed
asi.implementation_notes: Added 0002_vector_chunks migration and registered it; no sqlite-vss virtual table yet because extension-loading behavior should be tested separately

description: T14 adds vector chunk metadata inserts

asi.hypothesis: Store should be able to persist chunk metadata for claims before embedding vector indexing is connected
asi.red_evidence: add_vector_chunk_returns_chunk_id_for_claim failed because Store had no add_vector_chunk method
asi.green_evidence: cargo test --workspace -q passed; autoresearch.sh parsed tests_green=14 tests_total=14 and checks passed
asi.implementation_notes: Added add_vector_chunk using SQLite FK to claims and last_insert_rowid; no audit log entry because this is derived index metadata rather than a user memory write

description: T15 reads vector chunk metadata by id

asi.hypothesis: chunk metadata needs a typed read path before retrieval/indexing logic can depend on it
asi.red_evidence: get_vector_chunk_returns_claim_text_and_model failed because Store had no get_vector_chunk method
asi.green_evidence: cargo test --workspace -q passed; autoresearch.sh parsed tests_green=15 tests_total=15 and checks passed
asi.implementation_notes: Added VectorChunk DTO and get_vector_chunk query mapping id, claim_id, text, and embedding_model

description: T16 adds hardcoded HybridRAG weights

asi.hypothesis: v0.2 HybridRAG can start with ADR-0004's hardcoded alpha before adaptive query classification
asi.red_evidence: default_hybrid_weights_use_hardcoded_v0_2_alpha failed because memory_core::retrieval did not exist
asi.green_evidence: cargo test --workspace -q passed; autoresearch.sh parsed tests_green=16 tests_total=16 and checks passed
asi.implementation_notes: Added HybridWeights default alpha=0.65 plus vector_weight/graph_weight helpers; no scoring implementation yet

description: T17 blends vector and graph retrieval scores

asi.hypothesis: HybridWeights should encode ADR-0004's score formula directly so later retrieval can use one tested primitive
asi.red_evidence: hybrid_weights_blend_vector_and_graph_scores failed because HybridWeights had no blend method
asi.green_evidence: cargo test --workspace -q passed; autoresearch.sh parsed tests_green=17 tests_total=17 and checks passed
asi.implementation_notes: blend computes alpha*vector_score + (1-alpha)*graph_score; no ranking or retrieval side effects yet

description: T18 adds HybridRAG retrieval candidate scoring

asi.hypothesis: a typed RetrievalCandidate with precomputed hybrid score is the smallest bridge from score formula to ranked recall results
asi.red_evidence: retrieval_candidate_uses_hybrid_score_for_ordering_signal failed because RetrievalCandidate did not exist
asi.green_evidence: cargo test --workspace -q passed; autoresearch.sh parsed tests_green=18 tests_total=18 and checks passed
asi.implementation_notes: Candidate stores claim_id and blended score only; ordering/top-k deferred to a later TDD step

description: T19 ranks HybridRAG candidates by score

asi.hypothesis: ranked recall needs a deterministic descending score order primitive before Store-level HybridRAG can assemble results
asi.red_evidence: rank_candidates_orders_by_descending_hybrid_score failed because rank_candidates did not exist
asi.green_evidence: cargo test --workspace -q passed; autoresearch.sh parsed tests_green=19 tests_total=19 and checks passed
asi.implementation_notes: rank_candidates uses f64::total_cmp descending to avoid partial_cmp unwrap/NaN pitfalls

description: T20 limits HybridRAG candidates to top-k

asi.hypothesis: top-k truncation should be a tested pure primitive before wiring retrieval results into Store::recall
asi.red_evidence: top_k_candidates_returns_highest_scoring_limit failed because top_k_candidates did not exist
asi.green_evidence: cargo test --workspace -q passed; autoresearch.sh parsed tests_green=20 tests_total=20 and checks passed
asi.implementation_notes: top_k_candidates composes rank_candidates then truncate; deterministic with f64::total_cmp inherited from ranking

description: T21 vector backend default passed tests but failed clippy derivable_impls

asi.hypothesis: encode ADR-0006 default vector backend as sqlite-vss with Qdrant opt-in enum
asi.rollback_reason: clippy -D warnings rejected manual Default impl for VectorBackend as derivable_impls
asi.next_action_hint: reapply VectorBackend test and implementation using #[derive(Default)] plus #[default] on SqliteVss
asi.red_evidence: vector_backend_defaults_to_sqlite_vss failed because VectorBackend did not exist
asi.implementation_notes: No ADR/schema changes; only vector.rs enum and vector.rs test import/test were involved

description: T21 defaults vector backend to sqlite-vss

asi.hypothesis: ADR-0006's default sqlite-vss backend should be represented as a typed default with Qdrant only as opt-in
asi.red_evidence: vector_backend_defaults_to_sqlite_vss failed because VectorBackend did not exist
asi.green_evidence: cargo test --workspace -q passed; autoresearch.sh parsed tests_green=21 tests_total=21 and checks passed
asi.implementation_notes: Used #[derive(Default)] with #[default] SqliteVss after prior clippy derivable_impls failure; no schema or ADR changes

description: T22 parses qdrant vector backend opt-in

asi.hypothesis: ADR-0006's Qdrant escape hatch should be parseable explicitly while sqlite-vss remains the default
asi.red_evidence: vector_backend_parses_qdrant_opt_in failed because VectorBackend did not implement FromStr
asi.green_evidence: cargo test --workspace -q passed; autoresearch.sh parsed tests_green=22 tests_total=22 and checks passed
asi.implementation_notes: Added FromStr for sqlite-vss and qdrant with &'static str error; config/env plumbing deferred

description: T23 displays vector backend config values

asi.hypothesis: typed vector backends should round-trip to stable config strings before CLI/config surfaces expose them
asi.red_evidence: vector_backend_displays_config_values failed because VectorBackend did not implement Display/ToString
asi.green_evidence: cargo test --workspace -q passed; autoresearch.sh parsed tests_green=23 tests_total=23 and checks passed
asi.implementation_notes: Implemented Display matching FromStr strings for sqlite-vss and qdrant; no changes to parsing semantics

description: T24 defaults vector backend when config is unset

asi.hypothesis: config plumbing should treat an absent MEMORY_VECTOR_BACKEND-like value as sqlite-vss without requiring callers to duplicate default logic
asi.red_evidence: vector_backend_from_optional_config_defaults_when_unset failed because VectorBackend had no from_optional_config constructor
asi.green_evidence: cargo test --workspace -q passed; autoresearch.sh parsed tests_green=24 tests_total=24 and checks passed
asi.implementation_notes: Added pure Option<&str> parser to avoid mutating process environment in Rust 2024 tests

description: T25 trims vector backend config values

asi.hypothesis: backend config parsing should tolerate harmless surrounding whitespace from env/config files without changing accepted values
asi.red_evidence: vector_backend_config_parser_trims_whitespace failed with Err("unknown vector backend") for " qdrant "
asi.green_evidence: cargo test --workspace -q passed; autoresearch.sh parsed tests_green=25 tests_total=25 and checks passed
asi.implementation_notes: Trim only in from_optional_config; FromStr remains strict for canonical values

description: T26 parses Ollama response bodies via client

asi.hypothesis: the Ollama embedding client should own response-body parsing before actual HTTP transport is added
asi.red_evidence: ollama_embedding_client_parses_response_body_to_vector first failed because OllamaEmbeddingClient had no parse_response_body method; after implementation a malformed escaped JSON test string was corrected
asi.green_evidence: cargo test --workspace -q passed; autoresearch.sh parsed tests_green=26 tests_total=26 and checks passed
asi.implementation_notes: parse_response_body deserializes OllamaEmbeddingResponse and returns Vec<f32>; no network I/O added

description: T27 Ollama HTTP embed passed tests but failed clippy large error checks

asi.hypothesis: add a real local HTTP POST path for Ollama embeddings using ureq and a loopback test server
asi.rollback_reason: clippy -D warnings rejected EmbeddingError because ureq::Error made a large enum variant and Result large Err
asi.next_action_hint: reapply the HTTP embed test and implementation with boxed ureq::Error or a smaller string-based Http variant; also keep the test asserting only POST path because ureq may send body after initial header read
asi.red_evidence: ollama_embedding_client_posts_to_local_embeddings_endpoint first failed because OllamaEmbeddingClient had no embed method
asi.implementation_notes: Added workspace ureq dependency, EmbeddingError, and embed() posting JSON to /api/embeddings; cargo test passed before clippy failure

description: T27 posts embeddings requests to local Ollama HTTP endpoint

asi.hypothesis: v0.2 should include a real HTTP POST path to Ollama's /api/embeddings endpoint, verified against a local loopback server rather than external Ollama
asi.red_evidence: ollama_embedding_client_posts_to_local_embeddings_endpoint failed because OllamaEmbeddingClient had no embed method
asi.green_evidence: cargo test --workspace -q passed; autoresearch.sh parsed tests_green=27 tests_total=27 and checks passed
asi.implementation_notes: Added ureq dependency, EmbeddingError with string HTTP variant to avoid clippy large_enum_variant, and embed() that posts serialized request then parses response body; test only asserts POST path because body may arrive after initial header read

description: T28 lists vector chunks for a claim

asi.hypothesis: Store-level HybridRAG assembly needs a stable way to retrieve all chunk metadata for a claim before vector rows are scored
asi.red_evidence: list_vector_chunks_for_claim_orders_by_chunk_id failed because Store had no list_vector_chunks_for_claim method
asi.green_evidence: cargo test --workspace -q passed; autoresearch.sh parsed tests_green=28 tests_total=28 and checks passed
asi.implementation_notes: Added query ordered by vector_chunks.id; maps rows into existing VectorChunk DTO

description: T29 renders claim text for embedding chunks

asi.hypothesis: embedding/indexing should share a deterministic claim text rendering instead of callers hand-formatting subject/predicate/object triples
asi.red_evidence: claim_text_renders_subject_predicate_object_for_embedding failed because Claim had no text method
asi.green_evidence: cargo test --workspace -q passed; autoresearch.sh parsed tests_green=29 tests_total=29 and checks passed
asi.implementation_notes: Added Claim::text() returning "subject predicate object"; no storage or log behavior changed

description: T30 creates vector chunks from canonical claim text

asi.hypothesis: chunk creation should have a one-call path from claim id to canonical claim text so embedding/indexing callers cannot drift in text formatting
asi.red_evidence: add_vector_chunk_for_claim_uses_canonical_claim_text failed because Store had no add_vector_chunk_for_claim method
asi.green_evidence: cargo test --workspace -q passed; autoresearch.sh parsed tests_green=30 tests_total=30 and checks passed
asi.implementation_notes: add_vector_chunk_for_claim reuses get_claim + Claim::text + add_vector_chunk; no new log writes because chunk metadata remains derived index data

description: T31 tie-breaker change exposed flaky Ollama loopback test failure

asi.hypothesis: make HybridRAG candidate ranking deterministic by breaking equal score ties by claim_id
asi.rollback_reason: autoresearch.sh failed because previously kept ollama_embedding_client_posts_to_local_embeddings_endpoint intermittently returned ConnectionReset before completing response read
asi.next_action_hint: first stabilize the Ollama loopback test by reading the full HTTP request body before responding or adding Connection: close; then reapply the ranking tie-breaker test/change
asi.red_evidence: rank_candidates_breaks_score_ties_by_claim_id failed with order [2,1] vs expected [1,2] before implementation
asi.implementation_notes: Implementation used total_cmp(desc).then_with(claim_id asc) and cargo test --workspace passed once before autoresearch exposed the flaky vector HTTP test

description: T31 breaks HybridRAG ranking ties by claim id

asi.hypothesis: HybridRAG ranking should be deterministic even when candidates have identical scores, using claim_id as a stable tiebreaker
asi.red_evidence: rank_candidates_breaks_score_ties_by_claim_id failed with order [2,1] vs expected [1,2] before implementation
asi.green_evidence: cargo test --workspace -q passed; autoresearch.sh parsed tests_green=31 tests_total=31 and checks passed
asi.implementation_notes: Changed rank_candidates to score descending then claim_id ascending; also stabilized the prior Ollama loopback test response with Connection: close + flush after a crash exposed intermittent ConnectionReset

description: T32 validates HybridRAG alpha bounds

asi.hypothesis: caller-supplied HybridRAG alpha overrides should be validated to the unit interval before retrieval scoring uses them
asi.red_evidence: hybrid_weights_reject_alpha_outside_unit_interval failed because HybridWeights had no try_new constructor
asi.green_evidence: cargo test --workspace -q passed; autoresearch.sh parsed tests_green=32 tests_total=32 and checks passed
asi.implementation_notes: Added HybridWeights::try_new(alpha) returning Err for alpha <0 or >1; public alpha field left unchanged for existing tests/API, stricter construction can be adopted later

description: T33 parses HybridRAG alpha override strings

asi.hypothesis: CLI/config alpha overrides should parse into validated HybridWeights before retrieval scoring consumes them
asi.red_evidence: hybrid_weights_parse_alpha_override_from_string failed because HybridWeights did not implement FromStr
asi.green_evidence: cargo test --workspace -q passed; autoresearch.sh parsed tests_green=33 tests_total=33 and checks passed
asi.implementation_notes: Added HybridWeightsParseError with thiserror, changed try_new to return the typed error, and implemented FromStr via f64 parse plus unit-interval validation

description: T34 trims HybridRAG alpha override strings

asi.hypothesis: alpha override parsing should tolerate harmless surrounding whitespace from CLI/config inputs like vector backend parsing does
asi.red_evidence: hybrid_weights_parser_trims_alpha_override_whitespace failed with ParseFloatError Invalid for " 0.40 "
asi.green_evidence: cargo test --workspace -q passed; autoresearch.sh parsed tests_green=34 tests_total=34 and checks passed
asi.implementation_notes: Changed HybridWeights FromStr to trim before f64 parsing; validation semantics unchanged

description: T35 adds cosine similarity for vector scoring

asi.hypothesis: HybridRAG vector scoring needs a small deterministic cosine similarity primitive before retrieved embeddings can become RetrievalCandidates
asi.red_evidence: cosine_similarity_returns_one_for_identical_vectors failed because memory_core::vector::cosine_similarity did not exist
asi.green_evidence: cargo test --workspace -q passed; autoresearch.sh parsed tests_green=35 tests_total=35 and checks passed
asi.implementation_notes: Added cosine_similarity(&[f32], &[f32]) -> Option<f32> returning None for empty or mismatched lengths; zero-vector behavior not yet tested

description: T36 zero-vector cosine handling exposed flaky Ollama loopback header reset

asi.hypothesis: cosine_similarity should return None for zero vectors rather than Some(NaN)
asi.rollback_reason: autoresearch.sh failed because ollama_embedding_client_posts_to_local_embeddings_endpoint intermittently hit Connection reset by peer while reading headers despite cargo test passing once
asi.next_action_hint: stabilize the HTTP loopback test by reading the full request using Content-Length before writing the response and possibly shutdown write; then reapply zero-vector cosine test/change
asi.red_evidence: cosine_similarity_returns_none_for_zero_vector failed with left Some(NaN) right None before implementation
asi.implementation_notes: Implementation added a left_norm/right_norm zero check; cargo test --workspace passed before autoresearch exposed existing flaky HTTP test

description: T36 handles zero vectors in cosine similarity

asi.hypothesis: cosine_similarity should return None for zero vectors rather than Some(NaN) so vector retrieval does not rank invalid embeddings
asi.red_evidence: cosine_similarity_returns_none_for_zero_vector failed with left Some(NaN) right None before implementation
asi.green_evidence: cargo test --workspace -q passed; autoresearch.sh parsed tests_green=36 tests_total=36 and checks passed
asi.implementation_notes: Added zero-norm guard; also stabilized the Ollama loopback test by reading headers/body via Content-Length before writing the response, addressing the prior ConnectionReset crash
